title: hyptis alata subsp. rugosula
intro: Hyptis alata subsp. rugosula, commonly known as "Mexican tea" or "False Hyptis", is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as colds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Hyptis alata subsp. rugosula include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote relaxation, ease menstrual cramps, and soothe skin irritations.
